title: Bio - 2H/13C/13C Respiration CP 3D
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"23o30","text":"Purpose ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3o16f","text":"2H/13C/13C 3D correlation experiment using Respiration CP for 2H/13C transfer and RFDR for 13C/13C transfer. This experiment is used to mapping the site-specific dynamics for proteins.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4cjd0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c367j","text":"Scope ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5prok","text":"Site-specific dynamic measurement of protein.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"dou3b","text":"MAS rate: 10-20 kHz for 1.6 mm rotor","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7o34o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"49ns0","text":"Temperature:  Note that the temperature increases by 5 -10 °C due to frictional heating from spinning at 10-20 kHz for 1.6 mm rotors. Adjust the variable temperature (VT) control set point temperature accordingly to achieve desired sample temperature. For microcrystals and fibrils, typical sample temperature range is 0 to 10 °C, requiring a VT set point temperature of -10 to 0 °C. For membrane proteins and/or liposomes, the desired sample temperature depends on the phase transition of interest and can vary over a wide range depending on the sample.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bmnph","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6i2jt","text":"Below optimizations are required before setting up the hNH2d experiment. Example SOP for each optimization is attached at \"References\" section. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":32}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"611ah","text":"Calibrate 1H, 2H, and 13C solid pulses (p14, plw14, p13, plw13, p15, and plw15)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":10,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":14,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":22,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cvku9","text":"Optimize 2H-13C Respiration CP (p37, cnst37, p35, cnst31, cnst51, L1)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":9,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":12,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bd2vr","text":"Optimize low-power 1H decoupling (cpdprg4, p24, and cnst24)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":19,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bn8nj","text":"Optimize 13C/13C RFDR mixing (p36, L14, cpdprg7, p27, and cnst27)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":9,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":13,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8qilc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2uurp","text":"Setup time:  ~0.5 hours, presuming initial calibrations have already been completed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Load “RespirationCP3d-NAN” pulse program and parameter set “RespirationCP3d-NAN_par”.
Use Topspin command “edc” to open a new experiment.
Note that the parameters will be off if using different spectrometers/probes. Please consult to your facility manager/staff to get proper starting parameter set.
Set the optimized pulse parameter values. The optimizations that need to be done and parameters need to be updated are listed in the "Before start" section under "Guidelines & Warnings".
Set the 2 H carrier frequency (O3p) at ~0ppm, 1 H carrier frequency (O2p) at ~4ppm, and 13 C carrier frequency (O1p) at 40 ppm. For example, type “O1p” and enter, then input 40 to the opened window.
Direct dimension (t 3 or F3) Acquisition time (AQ, s): depending on 13 C T 2 , default is ~20ms. Spectral width (SW, ppm): ~400 ppm. Carrier frequency (O3p, ppm): 40 ppm.

Indirect 2 H dimension (t 1 or F1) Spectral width (SW, Hz): the 2 H dimension has spin sidebands at n*ω R (Hz, n≥1). So the spectral width should be able to cover most of the sidebands. The default n is 5, so the default spectral width is ~5ω R in Hz. Increment of delay (IN_F, s): coupled with spectral with. Increment of delay=1/SW. Carrier frequency (O1p, ppm): 0 ppm. Hypercomplex scheme (FnMODE): States-TPPI Maximum evolution time (AQ, s): The evolution time equals to (Increment of delay) * (TD). Once increment of delay is fixed as described above, change number of TD to adjust the total evolution time. The default value is ~0.25ms.

Indirect 13 C dimension (t 2 or F2) Spectral width (SW. ppm): For perdeuterated proteins, the region of interest is the aliphatic region, so the spectral width should cover from ~0 ppm to ~80ppm. The default spectral width is ~90 ppm. Increment of delay (IN_F, s): coupled with spectral with and must be rotor synchronized. For example, 50 µs for 20 kHz MAS spinning rate. Carrier frequency (O2p, ppm): 40 ppm for proteins. Hypercomplex scheme (FnMODE): States-TPPI Maximum evolution time (AQ, s): depending on 13 C T 2 , the default is ~10 ms. The evolution time equals to (Increment of delay)*(TD). Once increment of delay is fixed as described above, change number of TD to adjust the total evolution time.
Use recycle delay of 1.3*T 1 ( 2 H) for maximum sensitivity per unit time. Note that the 2 H T 1 is very short. The default value is 0.1s.
Due to the short recycle delay, only low-power 1 H decoupling is allowed, which should be sufficient for perdeuterated proteins.
Adjust measurement time as required by increasing number of scans in multiples of 4.
Start the experiment and monitor the first ~20-30 rows
Process first dimension FT to check for adequate signal correctly arraying indirect dimension.
creator: NMRFAM Facility
